factors.name: Fed Policy (Warsh debut)
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh held rates steady but signaled potential hike later in 2026; dot plot shifted to ~50% December hike probability (up from 30%). Removal of forward guidance creates volatility and supports higher USD, which pressures gold.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Hawkish Fed pivot and rate-hike expectations lift real yields, the single most correlated negative driver for gold. No specific TIPS level in news, but implied upward pressure from Warsh's tone and September/December hike odds.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD weakened post US-Iran MoU signing as risk sentiment improved; EUR/USD rallied to 1.1515, GBP/USD recovered from 2-month lows, AUD/USD above 0.7025. Weaker USD structure is BULLISH for gold, contradicting Fed hawkishness.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (US-Iran MoU)
factors.summary: US and Iran signed a memorandum of understanding to end war, including reopening of Strait of Hormuz and $300bn redevelopment package. This dramatically reduces Middle East geopolitical premium, lowering oil and reducing safe-haven demand for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: WTI holds above $75.10 but sentiment weak; IEA forecasts supply glut next year post-Iran deal; Goldman warns base case $80 Brent year-end. Lower oil = lower inflation expectations = less Fed hike urgency but also reduces inflation-hedge demand for gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Equity Market
factors.summary: Asia stocks surge (Nikkei, KOSPI at record highs); NZD, AUD, GBP all strengthen on improved risk sentiment post-Iran deal. Risk-on favors equities over safe-haven gold and weakens USD relative to commodity-linked currencies.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: No fresh CPI/PCE data in this window. Warsh's hawkish stance and uncertainty (no forward guidance) keeps 2026-28 inflation expectations elevated, but Iran peace and likely lower oil reduce near-term inflation impulse. Mixed signals.

factors.name: BoJ & Yen Carry
factors.summary: Japan's Kihara flags weak yen burden but offers no fresh intervention signal; USD/JPY consolidates above 160.00, highest since July 2024. BoJ remains dovish but intervention risk is present, creating volatility; unclear near-term direction.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Central Bank Buying
factors.summary: No fresh data on GLD, IAU holdings or PBoC/RBI buying in this window. Structural bid from central banks typically supportive, but not enough to counter hawkish Fed + risk-on dynamics.

factors.name: Price Action & Momentum
factors.summary: Gold slumped to near $4,250 post-FOMC (per FXStreet 23:18 report), now recovering to $4,339.70 (+0.27% in 4h, -0.15% in 24h). Move is shallow and choppy; initial selling from Warsh hawk signal has partially reversed on USD weakness and Iran peace.

screening.narrative: Gold faced a sharp selloff immediately post-FOMC when Warsh signaled potential rate hikes in 2H 2026 and removed forward guidance, pushing real yields higher and creating uncertainty. However, the US-Iran peace deal and subsequent risk-on sentiment have weakened the USD and lifted equity markets, partially offsetting the bearish Fed impulse. The micro-structure shows gold caught between hawkish Fed policy (bearish for gold, bullish for USD) and geopolitical de-escalation + improving risk sentiment (bullish for risk assets, bearish for safe-haven USD, bullish for gold via weaker dollar). This creates a shallow, choppy recovery from session lows—classic Phase 2 indecision.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI/Core CPI data (if scheduled in next 72h—check calendar for release date)
US Retail Sales / ISM Manufacturing / Services PMI
PBOC policy signals or yuan fixing pattern (currently allowing modest depreciation, 6.8130 vs 6.7752 estimate suggests soft weaken)
Japan intervention on USD/JPY if yen breaks lower again (risk factor, not baseline)
Fed speakers (Warsh, other FOMC members) elaborating on new framework and rate-path assumptions
Oil price action and OPEC+ response to Iran reopening/supply fears
screening.risk_warning: Invalidation of NEUTRAL bias: (1) If Fed speakers over next 48h walk back rate-hike signals or signal 'no hikes 2026', real yields would fall sharply and gold would rally decisively. (2) If Iran deal unravels or Netanyahu/Israeli escalation occurs, geopolitical premium snaps back, oil spikes, and safe-haven gold rallies. (3) If US economic data (NFP, ISM) disappoints materially, risk sentiment shifts to flight-to-safety, weakening dollar and supporting gold above $4,350. Conversely, if Fed hawkishness is reaffirmed and risk-on reverses, gold breaks $4,250 and tests $4,200.
screening.entry_guidance: Move extended 3+ hours post-FOMC with only shallow rebound; avoid chasing at current levels. Wait for retest of $4,280–$4,300 support or a break above $4,350 on fresh catalyst (e.g. soft inflation data or dovish Fed speaker) before entering with conviction. If risk sentiment fades and USD rebounds, support $4,250 is your next buy zone.
